name: sma
whatItMeasures: The SMA smooths out price data by calculating the average closing price over a set number of periods. The 50-day and 200-day SMAs are the most widely followed. When the shorter SMA crosses above the longer one (Golden Cross), it signals a potential uptrend; when it crosses below (Death Cross), it signals a potential downtrend. The 10-month SMA (roughly equivalent to the 200-day) is used as a long-term trend filter for monthly rebalancing strategies.

parameters.name: Short Period
parameters.rationale: The 50-day SMA captures the intermediate-term trend and is the most commonly referenced short-term moving average by institutional traders.

parameters.name: Long Period
parameters.rationale: The 200-day SMA is the standard benchmark for long-term trend direction, widely watched across all market participants.

howToRead: Price above the 200-day SMA indicates a long-term uptrend; below indicates a downtrend.
A Golden Cross (50-day crosses above 200-day) is a bullish signal suggesting momentum is shifting upward.
A Death Cross (50-day crosses below 200-day) is a bearish signal suggesting momentum is shifting downward.
The 10-month SMA rule: invest when the monthly close is above the 10-month SMA, move to cash or bonds when below.
SMA crossovers are lagging signals — they confirm trends rather than predict them.

signalRules.buy: Golden Cross: 50-day SMA crosses above 200-day SMA, or price closes above 200-day SMA.
signalRules.sell: Death Cross: 50-day SMA crosses below 200-day SMA, or price closes below 200-day SMA.
signalRules.neutral: Price oscillating around the 200-day SMA with no clear crossover; 50-day and 200-day are converging.

limitations: SMA crossovers are lagging indicators and can generate signals well after a trend has started.
In sideways or choppy markets, crossovers produce frequent whipsaws (false signals).
The Golden Cross and Death Cross are slow-moving — they work best in sustained trends, not short-term trading.
Equal weighting of all periods means the SMA reacts slowly to recent price changes compared to the EMA.
evidence: Meb Faber's research on the 10-month SMA timing rule (equivalent to the 200-day SMA) shows it delivers returns similar to buy-and-hold over long periods, but cuts maximum drawdown roughly in half — from approximately 55% to around 25%. The strategy's value is primarily in risk reduction rather than return enhancement. Golden Cross/Death Cross signals have been studied extensively and show reliability in trending markets but underperform in range-bound conditions.
